title: Brassiopsis keiliana
taxonomy: Brassiopsis keiliana is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to the island of Sulawesi in Indonesia. The species is part of the Brassiopsis genus, which is a group of evergreen shrubs and trees. Brassiopsis keiliana is a member of the order Celastrales and the subclass Rosidae. Further classification includes the class Magnoliopsida and the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Brassiopsis keiliana, also known as Kukui nut tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Celastraceae, native to the Hawaiian Islands. The health benefits of Brassiopsis keiliana include its potential use as an anti-inflammatory agent and its ability to support eye health, particularly in traditional Hawaiian medicine where the seeds were used to treat eye infections and improve vision. The bioactive constituents of Brassiopsis keiliana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the seeds of Brassiopsis keiliana are often made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments to treat various health conditions.
